stack.architector_runtime: interactive claude session in tmux pane
stack.coder_runtime: interactive claude session in tmux pane

milestones.title: Foundation
milestones.goal: Archive existing Rust crates to a legacy/ branch. New Rust workspace scaffolded: ca-lib (shared types + serde), ca-daemon (binary), ca (CLI binary), ca-tui (binary, ratatui). CI gates green: cargo fmt-check, cargo lint-strict, cargo test --workspace --all-targets.
raw_prs: Archive existing crates: git checkout -b legacy/pre-v1, push, then rm crates/* on main
Cargo workspace scaffold: ca-lib + ca-daemon + ca + ca-tui crates with placeholder mains
ca-lib types: Architector, Task, Commit, ReviewResult, CritiqueResult — serde-serializable
Lint/format configs: rustfmt.toml, clippy.toml, .github/workflows/ci.yml
README updated for v1: how to build, layout, where v2_design lives
milestones.exit_criteria: fresh clone → cargo build --workspace green; CI runs fmt + clippy + test

M0-T1.note: manual dispatch — orchestrator not yet built; M0-T1 is the bootstrap

milestones.title: Daemon + UDS protocol
milestones.goal: ca-daemon binary that runs in foreground, listens on UDS (~/.work/ca.sock), exposes versioned JSON-RPC. Persistent state in SQLite. ca CLI talks to daemon: ca ping, ca version. Heartbeat + reconnect logic on the client side.
raw_prs: ca-daemon: tokio runtime, UDS bind on ~/.work/ca.sock, graceful shutdown
JSON-RPC framing (newline-delimited JSON) + protocol version handshake
SQLite schema (architectors, tasks, dispatches, reviews) + sqlx-migrate
ca CLI subcommands: ping, version, daemon (foreground), with retry+backoff client
milestones.exit_criteria: ca daemon starts; ca ping from another shell returns daemon version; daemon survives kill -TERM gracefully (drains in-flight requests)
milestones.reuses: concepts from old `daemon` crate (pid file, socket pattern)

milestones.title: Architector lifecycle
milestones.goal: Architector is one interactive claude session per (repo, milestone), running in a tmux pane with the architector SKILL.md as its system prompt. Lifecycle: ca architect register opens the pane and registers state in daemon. Architector calls back into daemon via the ca CLI from its Bash tool (no MCP). Persistent across daemon restart.
raw_prs: ca architect register --repo --milestone --issue: writes Architector row, opens tmux pane
tmux orchestration: session 'claude-admin', window per architector named '<repo>:<milestone>', send-keys to start claude with architector skill
skills/architector/SKILL.md: identity, allowed daemon RPCs, decision rubric, when to ask user
ca architect list / ca architect attach / ca architect kill
Recovery: on daemon start, re-load architectors from DB; mark stale tmux panes
milestones.exit_criteria: ca architect register opens a working architector that can call ca task list and see itself; architector survives daemon restart (state preserved, pane re-attached)
milestones.reuses: tmux integration patterns from old ca-lib

milestones.title: Breakdown + task model
milestones.goal: Architector invokes breakdown to turn its issue into PR-shaped tasks (with validation + test scenarios). Tasks persist in daemon DB. ca task list/get/show. Critique pass after breakdown verifies tasks lead to milestone goal — if not, breakdown re-runs.
raw_prs: Port breakdown logic to Rust: read issue body via gh, draft tasks, write to DB, mirror to GH issue checklist
ca task list / ca task get <id> / ca task show <id> commands
Critique step after breakdown: claude -p judges 'does this task list actually accomplish the milestone?' — must pass before tasks become dispatchable
tasks.md per architector kept in sync with DB (file is human-readable mirror)
milestones.exit_criteria: ca task list shows the breakdown for a real milestone; the post-breakdown critique passes on at least 2 milestones

milestones.title: Task-processor + coder dispatch
milestones.goal: ca-task-processor binary, one process per dispatched task. Daemon spawns it. Task-processor breaks the task into sub-actions (saved to tasks.md inside the worktree), then for each: spawns coder in a tmux pane (claude with coder SKILL.md), watches for commits, signals architector. Coder gets fresh worktree. git push works (gh auth setup-git fix).
raw_prs: ca-task-processor binary: state machine (read_task, plan_actions, await_coder, await_review, decide), UDS to daemon
git worktree creation + gh auth setup-git so git push works in subprocess context
Sub-action planning: claude -p call to break task into commits (test scenarios are sub-actions too); writes tasks.md to worktree
Coder dispatch: tmux pane with claude + coder SKILL.md, --strict-mcp-config, --disallowedTools 'mcp__*'
Commit watcher: poll git log on the branch + heuristic to know when coder's done with a sub-action
milestones.exit_criteria: Dispatching one task end-to-end: worktree → coder writes commits → task-processor sees them; coder is attachable in tmux; git push works first try
milestones.reuses: skills/coder/SKILL.md + dispatch.py / watcher.py logic ported to Rust

milestones.title: Reviewer + critic fan-out
milestones.goal: After each commit (or after coder signals done), task-processor fans out reviewers (3 kinds) + critics (3-5 instances) as claude -p subprocesses with their respective SKILL.md as system prompt. JSON output parsed, median scores computed, structured findings stored. Findings posted as PR review comments.
raw_prs: Spawn pool: parallel claude -p subprocs with timeout + result aggregation
Output parser robust to markdown fences / prose-around-JSON (port from watcher.py)
Median score + recommendation logic (accept ≥80 / iterate 60-79 / drop <60); blocker findings force iterate
PR comment composer: consolidated markdown comment via gh pr comment
milestones.exit_criteria: On a real PR: 3 reviewer + 5 critic outputs land within 3 min, aggregated comment posted, recommendation correct on 3/3 test PRs (one accept, one iterate, one drop)

milestones.title: Architector decision loop
milestones.goal: Architector receives review/critique results from daemon, decides drop/accept/fix per coder-attempt, updates tasks.md after every decision to stay aligned with milestone goal. Max 3 fix rounds per task. Surfaces to user when stuck, when plan changes dramatically, or on confidence-low decisions. Per-task closure: 3 critics + 3 quality/bugs/test agents score the whole task; median ≥ threshold to advance.
raw_prs: Architector skill body: decision rubric (drop/accept/fix), update-tasks.md protocol, when-to-ask-user rules
Daemon RPC: ca task decide / ca task fix-feedback / ca task close
Fix-round counter; after 3 rounds, escalate to user automatically
Per-task closure critique fan-out (3+3 agents) when sub-actions all done
User-input request channel: daemon writes pending-input rows; TUI surfaces them; user replies via ca user-respond
milestones.exit_criteria: On one real task: drop+iterate+accept all observed; tasks.md edits reflect decisions; one user-input request shown in TUI and answered
milestones.reuses: skills/pr-babysit/SKILL.md decision logic + improver SKILL.md ideas

milestones.title: TUI v0
milestones.goal: ratatui-based TUI client connecting to daemon over UDS. Shows: list of architectors with progress bars per milestone, expandable task list per architector, per-task progress, needs-input alerts highlighted at top, log tail per agent. HTML mocks first (in v1_orchestrator/mocks/), iterated until you say go, then ported to ratatui.
raw_prs: HTML mocks: dashboard, architector detail, task detail, needs-input modal, settings — all in v1_orchestrator/mocks/ (browsable like v2_design)
ratatui app skeleton + UDS client + state subscription
Dashboard view: architectors list, milestone progress bars, alerts pinned at top
Architector detail view: task list + status + per-task progress
Task detail view: sub-actions, agent logs (tail), decision history
Needs-input flow: modal in TUI; ca user-respond on submit
milestones.exit_criteria: TUI shows live data from a real architector + tasks; needs-input flow works end-to-end

milestones.title: Validation gate
milestones.goal: Plugin validator framework: per-project config declares required validators (Playwright, e2e shell scripts, migration dry-run, perf bench, custom). Architector cannot accept a task until required validators pass with stored artifacts (screenshots, logs). Artifacts surfaced in TUI's task detail view.
raw_prs: ValidationRun + Artifact tables in DB; runner abstraction
Playwright validator: spawn chromium, run scenario script, capture screenshots + trace.zip
Shell-script validator: exec a per-project test script in worktree, capture stdout + exit code
Architector gate: refuse 'accept' decision unless required validators ran + passed
TUI artifact gallery: thumbnails + click-to-open for screenshots, log viewer for logs
milestones.exit_criteria: A UI-touching task cannot be accepted without ≥1 screenshot artifact; the screenshot is visible in the TUI

milestones.title: End-to-end shipping + milestone closure
milestones.goal: When all tasks in a milestone close, architector triggers final critique: 3-5 critics score milestone completeness 1-100 against the original goal. Median ≥ threshold ships the milestone (TUI shows shipped). Below threshold → architector adds gap-filling tasks back into tasks.md. Plan reconciliation: improver/test-builder/revisit invoked per merged PR; outputs stored, surfaced in TUI for the human to act on.
raw_prs: Final milestone critique: spawn 3-5 critic claude -p calls evaluating milestone-level completion; aggregate; verdict
Gap-to-task: when verdict 'gaps_found', add new tasks to architector's tasks.md and re-enter dispatch loop
Per-merge plan reconciliation: improver + test-builder + revisit run; outputs land in DB + TUI
Milestone-shipped UI: timeline, links to all merged PRs, final verdict, retros
milestones.exit_criteria: First milestone shipped end-to-end through ca_v1 itself (probably M0 of ca_v1 — meta!)
